mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an accommodative monetary policy that lowers discount rates, boosting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in periods of FEDFUNDS decline are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in payrolls (PAYEMS) and rising unemployment (UNRATE) signals labor market tightness with inflationary pressure, prompting central bank intervention that drives SPX returns negative over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns in co-moving PAYEMS/UNRATE regimes are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) or correlation between proxies and returns drops below 0.2.

mechanism_narrative: A 6-month decline in GDP (GDPC1) indicates weakening economic demand, reducing corporate earnings expectations and causing SPX returns to fall over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns in GDPC1 decline periods are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) or GDP growth reverses without corresponding equity performance.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent monthly CPI inflation above 2.5% (CPIAUCSL) triggers central bank rate hikes, increasing discount rates and producing negative SPX returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in CPI > 2.5% periods are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) or inflation expectations decouple from equity performance.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly drop in industrial production (INDPRO) signals reduced supply capacity, creating inflationary pressures that drive SPX returns negative over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns in INDPRO decline periods are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) or supply-side indicators diverge from equity performance.

mechanism_narrative: During high inflation (CPIAUCSL > 2.5%), monetary tightening (FEDFUNDS > 4.5%) amplifies equity risk premiums, causing SPX returns to fall over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in stagflation regimes are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) or interaction effect between CPI and rates disappears.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in residential construction (HOUST) and consumer spending (RSAFS) indicates robust demand, driving corporate profits and generating positive SPX returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in high HOUST/RSAFS regimes are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or demand proxies decouple from equity performance.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly payrolls growth above 100k (PAYEMS) signals strong labor market dynamics, increasing consumer spending and driving SPX returns positive over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns in high PAYEMS periods are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or payroll growth reverses without equity follow-through.
